Event: Refuses to go to hospital for a shot after severe allergic reaction
Explanation: Endangers his own health with no benefit and mildly stresses friends/medical staff.

Event: Suggests squirting the injection into his mouth like a squirt gun
Explanation: Replaces effective treatment idea with a useless one, delaying proper care.

Event: Crushes Monica’s hand during shot, causing bone bruise and puncture
Explanation: Reduces his own fear by over-squeezing while causing physical injury to Monica.

Event: Teases and bullies Monica in childhood
Explanation: Gains amusement and power while seriously harming Monica’s self-esteem.

Event: Aggressively threatens Chandler if he ever hurts Monica
Explanation: Protects Monica through a clear boundary and reinforces his protective role.

Event: Physically lays Chandler out to prove he’s serious about Monica
Explanation: Demonstrates resolve to safeguard Monica, briefly hurting Chandler to deter worse harm.

Event: Breaks his nose competing with Monica at childhood football
Explanation: Extreme competitiveness harms himself more than anyone else.

Event: Admits he always plays to win at poker even if others lose
Explanation: Pursues his own gain while explicitly disregarding others’ losses.

Event: Refuses to pay new building handyman’s retirement fund
Explanation: Saves his own money at the cost of the handyman’s welfare and social fallout.

Event: Doesn’t tell Rachel he loves her for entire first season
Explanation: His inaction costs him romantic timing and indirectly complicates his own love life.

Event: Leaves Ross’s confession to Rachel to slip out while Ross is in China
Explanation: Causes short-term chaos but helps Rachel realize her feelings and move plot forward.

Event: Becomes extremely jealous of Mark and disrupts Rachel’s work
Explanation: Jealous antics damage Rachel’s job and trigger breakup while also hurting himself.

Event: Convinces himself Emily will cheat in London and obsesses
Explanation: Jumps to paranoid conclusions that strain relationship and his own mental state.

Event: Pushes partners for closeness they’re not ready for
Explanation: Endangers multiple relationships without gaining lasting intimacy.

Event: Won’t allow partners time to cool off in arguments
Explanation: Escalates conflicts, worsening outcomes for everyone involved.

Event: Tries to flirt despite knowing he digs himself deeper
Explanation: Repeated awkward attempts mostly embarrass him and fail to win women over.

Event: Eats kiwi, triggering severe allergic reaction
Explanation: Serious harm to himself from carelessness; others only react protectively.

Event: Breaks a girl scout’s leg accidentally
Explanation: Unintentional but harms the child with no suggested offsetting gain.

Event: Goes out of his way to sell girl scout cookies to fund Space Camp
Explanation: Invests effort and social capital to try to help the injured girl.

Event: Builds a fake “space camp” with Joey and Chandler to cheer girl up
Explanation: Spends time and creativity to successfully make her happy with little for himself.

Event: Corrects people’s grammar and gets irate over mistakes
Explanation: Minor self-satisfaction at the cost of annoying and alienating his friends.

Event: Refuses to admit he smoked pot and makes up excuses
Explanation: Preserves fragile pride briefly while undermining trust and family harmony.

Event: Tries to convince Rachel to stay married so he avoids 3rd divorce
Explanation: Clings to status to protect ego while straining his relationship with Rachel.

Event: Lies to Rachel about getting their marriage annulled
Explanation: Endangers long-term trust for short-term avoidance of embarrassment.

Event: Gets uneven spray-on tan by botching the procedure repeatedly
Explanation: Bad instructions-following humiliates and harms his own appearance, amusing others.

Event: Waits for years pining for Rachel instead of building other ties
Explanation: Long-running fixation limits his own growth while others are mostly unaffected.

Event: Undermines relationships with Julie, Emily, Mona because of Rachel
Explanation: Sabotages stable relationships and hurts partners for a fantasy with no guaranteed payoff.

Event: Sells a young girl scout’s cookies after breaking her leg (re-attempt)
Explanation: Mobilizes his resources to directly support her goal of Space Camp.

Event: Overreacts with “MY SANDWICH?!” anger at coworker
Explanation: Outburst risks his professional image and relationships over minor food loss.

Event: Constantly whines about Rachel’s boyfriends and feels entitled to her
Explanation: Neediness harms his chances with Rachel and makes her life harder.

Event: Accidentally infects laptop with computer virus via porn link
Explanation: Chandler’s impulse viewing damages both his own and Ross’s equipment.

Event: Refuses to take responsibility for hurting Monica in childhood
Explanation: Protects ego at the cost of healing their relationship and Monica’s feelings.

Event: Argues Nevada is to blame for drunk marriage to Rachel, not him
Explanation: Deflection may feel good short-term but blocks self-improvement and accountability.

Event: Calls condom company to blame them for pregnancy warning
Explanation: Rant vents his frustration but wastes time and doesn’t change his situation.

Event: Often acts as a good, involved father to Ben
Explanation: Invests time and care that benefits both his child and his own fulfillment.

Event: Often acts as a good, involved father to Emma
Explanation: Stays engaged despite not being with the mother, helping Emma and deepening his bond.

Event: Uses his professional status to offer Zelner’s son a private museum tour
Explanation: Offers special, educational access that benefits the child and may help Rachel’s job.

Event: Makes joke offers that sound creepy then immediately self-corrects
Explanation: Quickly clarifies his intent, mitigating misunderstanding and salvaging the offer.

Event: Complains so much to Janice that even she breaks up with him
Explanation: Emotional venting costs him companionship but somewhat relieves his own pain.

Event: Improves behavior toward Monica and becomes close in adulthood
Explanation: Invests in a healthier sibling bond that benefits them both.
